slides.lead: Demonstration of the template. All copy is sample (dummy) content and exists only to showcase the styles.
slides.notes: Opening slide. Put the event title here, a one-line subtitle and a meta row at the bottom. Highlight one word with em for the accent color.

slides.lead: Five roles, one shared mission of better service.
slides.notes: Team slide. Uses colored initials instead of photos, so it needs no images.

slides.lead: Three parts, with room for questions in between.
slides.notes: Agenda as three large tiles. Good at the start of a block.

slides.lead: The state of services, data and the biggest friction points.
slides.notes: Divider slide between blocks. Large heading on a colored gradient.

slides.lead: People do not care which agency owns a form. They want a single, trusted place to get things done.
slides.footnote: // illustrative slide with an enlarged image
slides.notes: Hero slide: big heading, a strong illustration, a short caption. Good for a point or a chapter opener.

items: shorter time to complete a permit application online
verified accounts on the national identity portal
more services completed without an office visit
bullets: Measure outcomes for citizens, not internal activity.
Start where demand is highest and frustration is loudest.
A small pilot with a clear metric beats a grand plan without one.
slides.notes: KPI dashboard: three cards with an icon, a big number and a label, with supporting bullets below.

slides.lead: One secure login that every service can trust.
bullets: Identity once, with a strong assurance level.
Reuses that login across agencies and portals.
Personal data with consent and audit trails.
No repeated forms, no duplicate paperwork.
slides.notes: Two-column slide: bullets with a strong lead word on the left, illustration on the right. Text resizes itself.

slides.lead: The same service, two journeys and two cost curves.
cards: Citizens queue in person for every step. Slow and costly.
Download, print, post. Better, but still manual.
Apply, sign and pay online end to end. Lowest cost.
Digital by default, with help for those who need it.
slides.notes: A 2x2 grid of cards. Good for comparisons or listing variants.

slides.lead: Six typical building blocks of e-government.
cards: One trusted login across every public service.
Smart forms that pre-fill what the state already knows.
A single front door to find and start any service.
Citizens see where their request is, in real time.
Privacy by design with consent and audit logs.
Support channels so no one is left behind.
slides.notes: A 3x2 grid with an icon in each card header. Great for an overview of tools or capabilities.

items: Uptake levels off, so it pays to fix the basics first.
Digital uptake grows fast and reshapes counter workloads.
Proactive services act before citizens even apply.
slides.notes: Numbered cards with small trend charts and an illustration on the right. Ideal for three possible futures.

slides.lead: Five steps, each with a clear output.
slides.notes: A horizontal flow of steps with arrows. Each box has a title and a small caption.

slides.quote: Good government is not measured by the systems it owns, but by how quickly a citizen gets help.
slides.by: Sample quote to showcase the style
slides.notes: Quote slide with a bold left rule. Keep the attribution small.

slides.lead: Simple, but easy to forget under pressure.
bullets: Every service names the person accountable for it.
You know in advance how success is judged.
Critical services work for everyone, online or not.
slides.notes: A plain bullet slide with a strong lead word. Text scales up automatically to fill the space.

slides.lead: A portal no one trusts is a portal no one uses.
slides.notes: Statement slide: one large sentence centered on the page. Use it for a key message.

slides.lead: What to do this week, this month and this quarter.
slides.notes: Second divider, opening the practical part.

slides.lead: We switch to the portal and complete a real application live.
slides.footnote: // placeholder slide for a demo or break
slides.notes: Placeholder slide. Centered heading, ideal for a live demo, a break or questions.
Closing slide in the style of the title. Contact and a call to action.
